title: Celeste
edition_pinned: Celeste (Maddy Makes Games / Extremely OK Games, 25 January 2018 launch with Assist Mode as shipped) — Farewell DLC is optional post-content, not the pin.
moat: One air dash plus climb stamina frames every screen as a limited-resource precision solve, not a health fight.

loop_skeleton.description: Enter a screen, spend the single air dash and climb stamina to chain geometry, die on contact with hazards, respawn at the screen entrance with full resources, repeat until the screen exits.

depth_mechanisms.name: dash budget
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Madeline has one air dash that recharges on solid ground and from midair dash crystals. Screens are authored so the dash is the scarce commit that gates routes; diagonal eight-way dash is part of the legal input space.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If players receive unlimited air dashes by default, deaths-per-screen stop tracking screen geometry difficulty and the dash stops being the scarce commit.

depth_mechanisms.name: screen-scoped infinite retry
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Death restarts the current screen with full dash and stamina; progress is room-exit, not a lives currency. The mountain map chains hundreds of screens so failure cost is local time, not chapter restarts.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If death forces a multi-screen checkpoint rollback, completion rate for mid-game chapters drops because retry cost exceeds learning gain.

depth_mechanisms.name: assist mode opt-in break
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Assist Mode lets players change game speed, grant infinite dashes, invincibility, and other toggles without a separate story path. Designer intent treats Assist as breaking the fine-tuned difficulty by design so more players can finish.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If Assist Mode is removed and no equivalent toggles exist, players who need those options never reach the ending on the default ruleset.

feel_signature: Input commits a dash or stamina slice within one screen; failure is a full-resource retry at the screen door, so the decision is always the next frame sequence, not inventory or loadout.
second_session_answer: Players return to clear the next chapter screens or collect strawberries they skipped, using the same dash budget on harder geometry — not to spend meta currency.
packaging_lessons: Inference: ship chapter 1 with dash, climb, and screen-scoped retry before any collectible economy; level-design tooling centers on hundreds of precision stages.
Expose Assist toggles as player-owned breaks of the design, not as a hidden difficulty-tier flag that strips story.
anti_lessons: Do not bolt an XP or loadout layer onto a dash-budget platformer — it hides which inputs failed.
Do not replace screen-scoped retry with long checkpoint gaps; the depth is high death count at low time cost.

system_bom.note: run-jump-dash-climb-retry is the only required combat-free loop that advances screen geometry
death is the primary feedback; there is no lives counter — only local retry cost
screen and chapter progress persist so cleared geometry stays cleared while retries remain local; strawberries are optional persistence
dash-ready hair color and stamina bar must stay readable without menus so the scarce commit stays visible mid-air
chapter unlocks and B-sides gate harder geometry using the same verbs without new loadout systems

citations.claim: Edition pin: Celeste released 25 January 2018; Assist Mode is part of the shipped accessibility design.
Moat and dash budget: dash is central; climb contrasts as slower stamina-limited movement versus dash.
Dash recharges on ground and from midair crystals; dash is the scarce air commit.
Screen design tools for 300+ hardcore stages and mountain map arrangement (GDC Level Design Workshop).
Screen-scoped retry: death returns the player to the current room rather than a distant checkpoint; high death counts are expected.
Assist Mode: alter speed, infinite dashes, invincibility; designer framing treats Assist as breaking the game by design to empower players.
Assist Mode written coverage: optional accessibility settings ship with the game and preserve story access.
Feel signature: limited dash and stamina per screen; death resets the room resources for another attempt.
Second-session answer: strawberries and chapter progression are optional/collectible goals on the same movement verbs.
Save-state BOM: strawberries collect only after landing and persist as optional progress; screen progress is local until exit.
Save-state loop service: cleared rooms and chapter unlocks remain while deaths retry only the current room.
Win-loss-death BOM: no lives system; death count is tracked as feedback, not a fail currency.
UI BOM: Madeline's hair color signals dash readiness during play.
Progression BOM: B-Sides unlock harder remixed chapters using the same movement verbs.
Play-loop BOM: precision platforming with dash and climb is the core single-player structure.
Packaging lesson 1 (labeled Inference): dash, climb, and local retry are the chapter-1 vertical slice before collectible economy.
Packaging lesson 2: Assist toggles are player-owned breaks that keep story access.
Anti-lesson XP/loadout: the design centers on readable input failure on a fixed kit.
Anti-lesson long checkpoints: GDC tooling and death-local rooms keep failure cost low.
